description: The Office of Congresswoman Lori Trahan (MA-03) is currently accepting applications for the Spring 2021 remote internship program. The Washington, D.C. congressional intern responsibilities include answering phones, researching legislation, attending virtual hearings and briefings, and answering constituent letters on various issues before the House. Interns will also assist in various administrative tasks as needed. As a result, interns learn about the legislative process and the many other functions of a congressional office.
responsibilities: Answer constituent letters on various issues before the House
Assist in various administrative tasks as needed
qualifications: Be extremely motivated, flexible, and detail-oriented
Availability to intern from the beginning of January into May
District or Massachusetts ties preferred but not required
Strong oral and written communication skills
Ability to multi-task in a fast-paced work environment
Interest in public service
how_to_apply: The deadline for applications is November 15th. To apply, please complete the web form at https://trahan.house.gov/forms/internships/ and email your Cover Letter (PDF) and Resume (PDF) to Reecha Patel (Reecha.Patel@mail.house.gov) with subject line indicating D.C. Office-General Internship.
equal_opportunity: Candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description: The Office of Congresswoman Lori Trahan (MA-03) is currently accepting applications for the Spring 2021 remote internship program. The Washington, D.C. press intern will assist the driven press team. Interns will work closely with communications staffers and learn about the press operation of a congressional office.
responsibilities: Assist the team with a variety of projects as needed
qualifications: Be extremely motivated, flexible, and detail-oriented
Availability to intern from the beginning of January into May
District or Massachusetts ties preferred but not required
Strong oral and written communication skills
Ability to multi-task in a fast-paced work environment
Interest in public service
Foundation of technical knowledge on social media platforms
Experience in studying Communications, Journalism, or Public Relations is a plus
how_to_apply: The deadline for applications is November 15th. To apply, please complete the web form at https://trahan.house.gov/forms/internships/ and email your Cover Letter (PDF) and Resume (PDF) to David Todisco (David.Todisco@mail.house.gov) with subject line indicating D.C. Office-Press Internship. Submission of a sample graphic with application materials is recommended but not required.
equal_opportunity: Candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description: The Office of Congresswoman Lori Trahan (MA-03) is currently accepting applications for the Spring 2021 remote internship program. The District Office intern responsibilities include answering phones, answering constituent letters on various issues, assisting with resolving constituent casework, researching daily press clips, and drafting memos as needed. Interns will gain a comprehensive knowledge of Massachusetts' 3rd District, as well as the services the District Office provides.
responsibilities: Answer constituent letters on various issues
qualifications: Be extremely motivated, flexible, and detail-oriented
Availability to intern from the beginning of January into May
District or Massachusetts ties preferred but not required
Strong oral and written communication skills
Ability to multi-task in a fast-paced work environment
Interest in public service
how_to_apply: The deadline for applications is November 15th. To apply, please complete the web form at https://trahan.house.gov/forms/internships/ and email your Cover Letter (PDF) and Resume (PDF) to Jack Brindisi (Jack.Brindisi@mail.house.gov) with subject line indicating District Office Internship.
equal_opportunity: Candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description:
The Office of Congresswoman Brenda L. Lawrence (MI-14) seeks motivated, detail-oriented, and energetic professionals for remote internships for the Washington, DC office.

Qualified applicants should have strong communications skills, the ability to manage multiple tasks, possess strong writing skills, and demonstrate a solid work ethic. Interns will be provided with computers to complete official work.

Full-time and part-time internship positions are available beginning in January 2021.
responsibilities: Drafting constituent correspondence on a variety of issues
Assisting with drafting talking points and press releases
qualifications: Strong communications skills
Ability to manage multiple tasks
Strong writing skills
Solid work ethic
how_to_apply:
To apply, please send a resume, cover letter, and brief writing sample to Ryan.Casman@mail.house.gov with the phrase "Internship Application" in the subject line.

Please indicate expected start and end dates as well as hours available to work in your cover letter.

description:
The Washington, D.C. Office of Congressman Steve Cohen (D-TN-09) is seeking interns for spring 2021. This position is remote. If the public health situation changes significantly, the position could transition to in-office.

This position offers an opportunity to learn about the U.S. House of Representatives and gain substantive work experience in an exciting and fast-paced office. Internships are offered on a full-time and part-time basis, and stipends are offered based on need and ties to Tennessee's 9th District. Interns will work for 10-16 weeks (January-April). Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.
responsibilities: Assist with constituent correspondence and draft email blasts and other forms of outreach
Attend virtual briefings and track virtual hearings
Conduct legislative research on an as-needed basis
qualifications: Demonstrated interest in politics, policy and/or public service
Strong communications and writing skills
Hard-working, dependable, detail-oriented and professional
Self-motivated (especially given that all work is remote)
Ties to Tennessee's 9th District are a bonus, but not required
how_to_apply: To apply, applicants must complete the form and submit the following materials on https://cohen.house.gov/services/internships: Resume, Cover letter, Three references.
salary_info: Stipends are offered based on need and ties to Tennessee's 9th District

description:
The Office of Congressman Mark Pocan (D-WI) is seeking a Full-time Virtual Intern to begin at the end of December or beginning of January.

While an intern will work mainly with D.C.-based staff, applicants can be located in the D.C./Maryland/Virginia (DMV) area OR in the Congressman's congressional district (WI-02) during their tenure.

Interns in our office perform legislative and administrative work while gaining valuable career and educational experience.
responsibilities: Support staff members on various projects of interest
qualifications: Organized and detail-oriented
Responsible
Willing to learn
Able to work cooperatively
Possess strong written communication skills
Committed to public service
how_to_apply:
To apply, please send a resume, cover letter, and a brief writing sample to Sydney.Scott@mail.house.gov with "Virtual Pocan Internship 2021" in the subject line.

Interested applicants should also apply to the scholarship program at: https://pocan.house.gov/scholarship-program

description: The Office of Congresswoman Gwen Moore (WI-04) is seeking a virtual press intern to assist with a fast-paced communications operation for Spring 2021. This is a paid position.
qualifications: Strong writing, editing, event planning, social media, graphic design, and photography skills
Ability to work on tight deadlines
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a cover letter, resume, short writing sample, and dates of availability to GwenMoorePress.Moore@mail.house.gov with "Spring 2021 Press Internship" in the subject line. We also recommend that applicants submit 1-3 sample graphics.
equal_opportunity: Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description:
The Washington D.C. office of Congressman Ken Buck (CO-04) is currently accepting internship applications for the Spring of 2021.

Due to the ongoing spread of COVID-19, some of these duties may be significantly altered. The ideal candidate for this paid internship will be team- and detail-oriented and have strong written and verbal communication skills.

Students and recent graduates interested in learning more about our country's legislative process are encouraged to apply. The Congressman sits on the Judiciary Committee and the Foreign Affairs Committee, making this an ideal opportunity for those interested in law and/or foreign policy. Colorado ties are preferred but by no means required.
responsibilities: Research legislation for the Member and legislative staff
Answer constituent letters on a variety of issues
qualifications: Team- and detail-oriented
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Students and recent graduates interested in learning more about the legislative process
Colorado ties preferred
how_to_apply: Email resume, cover letter, and a brief writing sample to Caleb Kostreva at internships.co04@mail.house.gov with the subject line "Spring 2021 Internship." Please send all attachments as PDFs.

position_title: Labor Policy Intern or Labor Policy Fellow
office: Committee on Education and Labor, Majority
description: The Committee on Education and Labor, Majority is currently seeking a second or third year graduate or law student as a 'Labor Policy Intern' or 'Labor Policy Fellow' for the spring of 2020. Interns and Fellows perform a variety of tasks that support the staff in our legislative work.
responsibilities: Conduct legislative research, legal research, and fact checking
Perform technical writing including legislation, memos, and speeches
Assist staff in hearing and markup preparation and execution
Attend hearings, markups, briefings, and meetings to inform staff and Members as they work to craft legislation and policy proposals
qualifications: Second or third year graduate or law student
Ability to start immediately and commit through May
Demonstrate study, practical, or policy experience in issues relevant to the Committee
Fellows must be participating in an established fellowship program
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email their cover letter, resume and internship applicant information form to E&L_Interns&Fellows@mail.house.gov. Please, also, include two short, relevant writing samples in your application materials. Applicants may visit our website at https://edlabor.house.gov/about/internships for more information.
